subjects.name: English Language (Prelims & Mains)
subjects.analysis: Reading Comprehension (RC) dominates. High-level inference and contextual vocabulary are essential for PO-level difficulty.

subjects.name: Quantitative Aptitude / Data Analysis
subjects.analysis: Transition from calculation-heavy Prelims to concept-heavy Data Analysis in Mains. Arithmetic remains the backbone.

subjects.name: Reasoning & Computer Aptitude
subjects.analysis: Puzzle-centric with a focus on logical flow. Mains level introduces high-level critical reasoning and coded logic.

subjects.name: General & Banking Awareness (Mains)
subjects.analysis: Focus on the last 6 months of current affairs with a deep dive into RBI circulars and financial terminology.

subjects.name: Descriptive Test (Mains Only)
subjects.analysis: Online typing test assessing professional writing and formal communication.

mentorship_data.title: Prelims Strategy: The Filter Phase
mentorship_data.content: Accuracy is more important than raw attempts. Clear the sectional cutoffs first by picking easy arithmetic and direct puzzles. Speed Math is your best friend.

mentorship_data.title: Mains Strategy: The Merit Phase
mentorship_data.content: General Awareness and Data Interpretation are the highest weightage sections. You must start GA preparation 6 months prior to the exam date for a safe score.

mentorship_data.title: Interview & Personality
mentorship_data.content: Banking awareness and current affairs are strictly required. Work on body language and your personal introduction to score in the 80+ range in the interview.

mentorship_data.title: Descriptive Writing Practice
mentorship_data.content: Use a standard keyboard for typing practice. Focus on word limits and error-free grammar to qualify comfortably.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there an interview in IBPS PO?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes, a personal interview is the final stage of the selection process after clearing Mains.

frequently_asked_questions.q: What is the negative marking policy?
frequently_asked_questions.a: A penalty of 0.25 marks is deducted for every incorrect response in both objective phases.

frequently_asked_questions.q: How is the final merit list calculated?
frequently_asked_questions.a: The final result is based on the combined score of Phase II (Mains) and Phase III (Interview) in an 80:20 ratio.
Yes, it is a mandatory qualifying section held immediately after the objective Mains exam.
Yes, candidates must score above the minimum qualifying marks in each individual section as well as the overall total.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is GA important for the interview?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Very important. Most interview questions revolve around banking policies, economic trends, and current affairs.
